source_intake_record.raw_question_text: In the power system shown in the figure, it is known that \( U = 10 \, \text{kV} \), and the capacitive current flowing through the grounding point during a single-phase ground fault is 35 A. To compensate the current flowing through the grounding point during a single-phase ground fault to 20 A, the inductance of the required arc suppression coil should be:
